evidence.claim: Implemented RAG-based customer support chatbot using OpenAI embeddings, Pinecone, and GPT-4/fine-tuned models
evidence.source_text: Implemented a RAG-based customer support chatbot integrated with our existing ticketing system. Built the document ingestion pipeline (chunking, embedding via OpenAI embeddings, storing in Pinecone) and the answer-generation layer (initially GPT-4, then a fine-tuned smaller model for cost control).

evidence.claim: Designed evaluation framework for RAG using BLEU, ROUGE, and human-in-the-loop scores
evidence.source_text: Designed the evaluation framework with both automatic metrics (BLEU, ROUGE) and human-in-the-loop quality scores.

evidence.claim: RAG deployment reduced average ticket resolution time by 31%
evidence.source_text: Deployment cut average ticket resolution time by 31% for the supported categories.

evidence.claim: Evolved e-commerce search ranking from hand-tuned to learning-to-rank model
evidence.source_text: Owned the ranking layer for an e-commerce search product, evolving it from a hand-tuned scoring function to a learning-to-rank model over 9 months.

evidence.claim: Improved revenue-per-search by 12% via ranking model
Developed semantic search for ~500K documents using sentence-transformers and FAISS
evidence.source_text: Developed a semantic search feature for an internal knowledge base of ~500K documents. Used sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 initially, later upgraded to bge-base) with FAISS for fast nearest-neighbor retrieval.

evidence.claim: Improved search-relevance by 35% over Elasticsearch BM25
evidence.source_text: Reported search-relevance improvement of 35% over the prior Elasticsearch BM25 setup, validated through human relevance judgments.

evidence.claim: Proficient in a wide range of ML tools including Python, OpenCV, scikit-learn, QLoRA, PEFT, Pinecone, Weaviate, MLflow, and pgvector
evidence.source_text: Skills: OpenCV... scikit-learn... QLoRA... PEFT... Pinecone... Weaviate... Python... MLflow... pgvector

evidence.claim: Experience with operational ML including A/B testing, drift monitoring, and retraining schedules
evidence.source_text: Along the way I've gotten comfortable with the operational side — A/B testing, drift monitoring, retraining schedules.

evidence.claim: Values learning through shipping real systems and production load testing
evidence.source_text: my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.

evidence.claim: Focuses on infrastructure and data quality as critical components of ML engineering
evidence.source_text: Most of the work was infrastructure and data quality — the modeling part was almost the easy bit.
